title: The Stolen Sandwich
case: At 12:30 PM, someone stole Margot's labeled tuna sandwich from the office kitchen fridge. Margot is furious and wants answers.

suspects.name: Raj
suspects.alibi: I was in the conference room for the 12-1 team lunch meeting. Pizza was served — I had two slices.

suspects.name: Lena
suspects.alibi: I went to Sweetgreen for my usual kale salad. I didn't get back to the office until 1:15.

suspects.name: Ben
suspects.alibi: I was at my desk eating pad thai leftovers. I heated them in the microwave around 12:25.

suspects.name: Priya
suspects.alibi: I was out on my lunchtime walk listening to a podcast. I didn't eat lunch today.

suspects.name: Dex
suspects.alibi: I was in the kitchen around 12:20 making a cup of tea. I remember seeing the tuna sandwich was still there.

starter: The office microwave has a digital log. Its last use was at 12:05 PM — it was not used again until 1:40 PM.
Security footage captured a person in a gray hoodie entering the kitchen at 12:32 PM and leaving with a wrapped sandwich.
Only two people in the office were wearing gray hoodies today: Ben and Dex. Dex's keycard shows he entered the kitchen at 12:20 and left at 12:23 — before the theft.

exonerations.raj: Raj's team meeting was confirmed by four attendees — he was in the conference room from 12 to 1 PM without leaving.
exonerations.lena: Lena's Sweetgreen receipt is timestamped 12:48. She couldn't have been in the office at 12:32.
exonerations.priya: Priya's podcast app shows continuous playback from 11:55 to 1:00 PM, and her phone's GPS puts her three blocks from the office during the theft window.
exonerations.dex: Dex's keycard shows he was in the kitchen from 12:20 to 12:23 — seven minutes before the theft at 12:30.

reveal: Ben said he heated pad thai in the microwave at 12:25. But Clue 1 shows the microwave's last use was at 12:05 PM — Ben's story is a lie. Clue 2 puts a gray-hooded person in the kitchen at 12:32 taking a sandwich, and Clue 3 narrows that to Ben or Dex — with Dex ruled out by his 12:20–12:23 keycard entry. Ben was the hooded figure.

title: The Missing Umbrella
case: At 4:15 PM on a rainy Tuesday, someone took Evelyn's distinctive red umbrella from the lobby umbrella stand at her apartment building. She needs it back before her dinner reservation.

suspects.name: Mrs. Chen
suspects.alibi: I was in my apartment watching my show. I haven't been down to the lobby since I got back from groceries this morning.

suspects.name: Jake
suspects.alibi: I was recording an episode in my home studio from 3 to 5. My guest can verify — we didn't take a break.

suspects.name: Marcus
suspects.alibi: I walked my dog Pepper at 4. We were out for about 30 minutes and came back soaked.

suspects.name: Sofia
suspects.alibi: I was studying at the coffee shop across the street. I came back around 4:30 and went straight to my apartment.

suspects.name: Derek
suspects.alibi: I was fixing the radiator in 2B from 3:45 to 4:30. Marcus wasn't home — I had to let myself in.

starter: The lobby camera shows someone carrying Evelyn's red umbrella leaving the building at 4:16 PM.
It has been raining steadily since 2 PM.
Marcus's dog Pepper is terrified of rain — Marcus posted in the building chat last week that Pepper refuses to step outside in even a drizzle.

exonerations.mrs-chen: Mrs. Chen's TV was on all afternoon; her downstairs neighbor confirmed hearing the exact show that airs 3 to 5 PM continuously.
exonerations.jake: Jake's podcast guest confirmed an unbroken recording session from 3 to 5 PM — no bathroom breaks, no pauses.
exonerations.sofia: Sofia's coffee shop receipt is timestamped 4:28 PM, and the cashier confirms she was at her laptop from 2:30 to 4:30.
exonerations.derek: Derek's building keycard shows continuous presence inside Apt 2B from 3:45 to 4:30, and the tenant of 2B was home to let him out.

reveal: Marcus said he walked Pepper at 4 PM in the rain and came back soaked. But Clue 3 is clear: Pepper refuses to go outside in rain — Marcus announced this himself in the building chat. Clue 2 confirms it was raining steadily all afternoon, so Marcus's whole story is a lie. Clue 1 shows someone took the red umbrella at 4:16 — Marcus himself, ducking out while pretending to be on a walk.

title: The Vanishing Cake
case: Someone ate the last slice of Nora's birthday cake from the break room fridge overnight. It was there at 6 PM when Nora left, and gone by 7 AM when she arrived.

suspects.name: Carlos
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk all night. I did my rounds at 10 PM and 2 AM but I never went into the break room.

suspects.name: Tamara
suspects.alibi: I cleaned the third floor from 8 to 10 PM. The break room is on the second floor — that's Jess's zone.

suspects.name: Owen
suspects.alibi: I stayed late debugging a server issue. I was at my desk until midnight and ordered pizza delivery around 9.

suspects.name: Jess
suspects.alibi: I cleaned the second floor from 8 to 10 PM. I saw the cake in the fridge but I didn't touch it.

suspects.name: Maya
suspects.alibi: I left at 5:30 PM and didn't come back. I was at a concert downtown — I have the ticket stub.

starter: The break room keycard log shows three door entries between 6 PM and 7 AM: at 8:12 PM, 8:47 PM, and 2:04 AM.
After midnight, Carlos was the only person in the building — every other badge had exited. His security round log shows he badged the second-floor hallway checkpoint at 2:03 AM.
The break room sink contained a fork with chocolate frosting residue. The dishwasher was empty and clean.

exonerations.tamara: Tamara's floor-cleaning checklist and timestamped photos confirm she was on the third floor, not the second, from 8 to 10 PM.
exonerations.owen: Owen's badge shows he left the building at 11:58 PM and didn't return — he was home before the 2:04 AM entry.
exonerations.jess: Jess's cleaning checklist requires all utensils go in the dishwasher. The fork in the sink is the opposite of her routine — she wouldn't have left it there.
exonerations.maya: Maya's concert ticket was scanned at 7:45 PM downtown, a 40-minute drive from the office. She didn't return to the building.

reveal: Carlos said he never went into the break room. But Clue 1 shows someone opened the break room door at 2:04 AM, and Clue 2 places Carlos as the only person in the building at that hour — with his badge hitting the second-floor checkpoint at 2:03 AM, one minute before the break room door opened. Clue 3 shows a fork abandoned in the sink, consistent with someone grabbing a quick bite and leaving no trace.

title: The Library Book Bandit
case: A signed first edition of 'Dune' vanished from the locked display case at the Millbrook Public Library sometime between 2 PM and closing at 5 PM. The case was found unlocked.

suspects.name: Helen
suspects.alibi: I was running the children's reading hour from 2 to 3, then at the front desk until close. I have the only key to the display case.

suspects.name: Victor
suspects.alibi: I was browsing the sci-fi section from about 1:30 to 3. I left through the front door and Helen checked me out.

suspects.name: Dana
suspects.alibi: I was shelving returns in the nonfiction wing all afternoon. I never went near the display case area.

suspects.name: Keith
suspects.alibi: I was fixing a leaky pipe in the basement from 1 to 4 PM. You can check — the floor was still wet when I left.

suspects.name: Suki
suspects.alibi: I was studying at my usual table by the windows from noon to 4. I had my headphones on the whole time.

starter: The library's front door sensor log shows Victor exited at 3:12 PM — not at 3 PM as he claimed.
Helen's display case key was missing from her desk drawer at 4 PM and later found on the floor in the sci-fi section, on the path to the front door.
Between 3:00 and 3:15 PM, Victor was the only patron in the sci-fi section — the display case is in the alcove directly off that aisle.

exonerations.helen: Helen ran the children's reading hour from 2 to 3, then stayed at the front desk visible to six patrons and the library assistant until close.
exonerations.dana: Dana's return-cart camera timestamped her continuously in the nonfiction wing from 2 to 4:30 PM.
exonerations.keith: Keith's basement work order was logged by the plumbing company's dispatch — he was in the basement the whole afternoon, with a wet floor to prove it.
exonerations.suki: Suki was visible at her window table to both Helen and the library assistant from noon to 4 PM — she never left her seat.

reveal: Victor said he left at 3 PM and that Helen checked him out. But Clue 1 shows he actually exited at 3:12 — twelve unaccounted minutes. Clue 3 places him alone in the sci-fi aisle during that exact window, right next to the display case. Clue 2 shows the key he used was dropped in that same aisle on his way out. Victor lifted the key while Helen was distracted greeting returning readers, opened the case, took the book, and slipped out.

title: The Garden Gnome Caper
case: Mr. Patterson's prized garden gnome 'Gerald' disappeared from his front yard sometime between 10 PM last night and 6 AM this morning. Gerald has won 'Best Lawn Ornament' three years running.

suspects.name: Linda
suspects.alibi: I was watching TV until midnight and then went to bed. I didn't hear anything unusual.

suspects.name: Tom
suspects.alibi: I walk dogs in the neighborhood every evening. I finished my last walk at 9:30 and went straight home.

suspects.name: Wendy
suspects.alibi: I was at the HOA board meeting at the community center until 11 PM, then drove straight home. Three board members can confirm.

suspects.name: Brett
suspects.alibi: I was up late gaming online with my friends until about 2 AM. My game logs will show I was active the whole time.

suspects.name: Diane
suspects.alibi: I was asleep by 9 PM. I take melatonin every night and I'm a very heavy sleeper. My husband can confirm.

starter: Mr. Patterson's Ring doorbell camera was offline from 11:45 PM to 12:10 AM due to a Wi-Fi outage.
Mr. Patterson and Linda share a router — they live in a duplex and split one Wi-Fi connection. The outage affected only their two units. Linda would have known the camera was dark.
Fresh wheelbarrow tracks in the soft soil next to Mr. Patterson's lawn lead directly to Linda's side of the duplex.

exonerations.tom: Tom's last dog-walking client confirmed dropoff at 9:25 PM three blocks away, and his car's GPS shows it never left his driveway after 9:40 PM.
exonerations.wendy: Wendy's HOA meeting attendance and the community center's alarm log confirm she was there until 11:05 PM, and her car's GPS shows her home by 11:20 — after which she stayed put.
exonerations.brett: Brett's gaming session logs confirm continuous input activity from 10:30 PM to 2:15 AM with no breaks longer than 2 minutes — not enough time to cross the street and back.
exonerations.diane: Diane's smart watch sleep log shows uninterrupted sleep from 8:55 PM to 6:40 AM — her heart rate stayed in deep-sleep range all night.

reveal: The theft happened during the Wi-Fi outage that knocked out Mr. Patterson's Ring camera (Clue 1). Clue 2 is the smoking gun: Linda shares a router with Mr. Patterson, so she — and only she — knew exactly when the camera would be dark. Clue 3 shows wheelbarrow tracks from Mr. Patterson's lawn leading straight to Linda's side of the duplex. Motivated, informed, and with a trail of evidence, Linda took Gerald during her manufactured window.

title: The Coffee Shop Tip Jar
case: The tip jar at Brewed Awakening disappeared during the morning rush between 8 and 9 AM. It had roughly $85 in cash and was sitting on the counter by the register.

suspects.name: Nina
suspects.alibi: I was behind the espresso machine the whole rush. I didn't step out from behind the counter once — we were slammed.

suspects.name: Ravi
suspects.alibi: I came in at 8:15, ordered my usual cortado, and sat in the corner booth reading until 9. I pay with my phone.

suspects.name: Greta
suspects.alibi: I picked up a batch of online orders at 8:30. I was in and out in two minutes — I had six more stops.

suspects.name: Frank
suspects.alibi: I clocked in at 8:45 and went straight to stocking the pastry case. The tip jar was already gone when I noticed at 9.

suspects.name: Zoe
suspects.alibi: I was shadowing Nina and learning the register from 7:30 to 9. I was right next to the counter the entire time.

starter: The security camera covering the register was bumped off its angle at 8:18 AM — up until that minute it was pointing at the counter; after, it was pointing at the ceiling.
The tip jar was last confirmed at 8:15 AM by a customer's Instagram photo. Only staff were behind the counter between 8:15 and the end of the rush.
Nina spent the entire rush at the espresso machine at the far end of the counter, six feet from the register. Zoe — the trainee — was the one actually stationed at the register.

exonerations.nina: Nina's espresso machine has a per-drink log — she pulled 34 shots between 8:00 and 9:00, with no gap longer than 45 seconds. She never stepped away.
exonerations.ravi: Ravi's corner booth is eight feet from the counter with a bench in between. Three other regulars confirm he was reading there from 8:15 to 9, and his order was rung up at 8:16.
exonerations.greta: Greta's delivery app GPS log shows she arrived at 8:32 AM and left at 8:34 AM — well after the camera was bumped at 8:18.
exonerations.frank: Frank's timecard confirms he badged in through the back entrance at 8:47 AM, nearly half an hour after the camera was tampered with.

reveal: Clue 1 pinpoints the moment the camera was bumped: 8:18 AM. Clue 2 narrows the suspect pool to staff only in that window. Clue 3 is the smoking gun: Nina was locked to the espresso machine six feet away, so the only staff member at the register was Zoe — the trainee who claimed she was 'shadowing Nina' but was actually manning the register alone. Zoe bumped the camera and pocketed the jar.

title: The Parking Lot Scratch
case: Someone keyed a long scratch down the side of Dr. Patel's new Tesla in the hospital parking garage between 7 AM and 3 PM. She's checked the garage cameras but the angle doesn't cover her spot.

suspects.name: Nurse Kim
suspects.alibi: I was on shift in the ER from 6 AM to 2 PM. I parked on level 2, same as always. I didn't go back to my car until my shift ended.

suspects.name: Gary
suspects.alibi: I was at the booth at the garage entrance all day. I see every car come in and out but I don't walk the levels.

suspects.name: Dr. Mason
suspects.alibi: I was in surgery from 8 AM to noon, then in my office doing paperwork until 4. I parked on level 3.

suspects.name: Phil
suspects.alibi: I arrived at 9 AM to visit my wife and left at 1 PM. I parked on level 1 near the elevator.

suspects.name: Angela
suspects.alibi: I was in back-to-back meetings from 8 to 3. I parked in the reserved spots on level 2, right next to Dr. Patel.

starter: The reserved parking section on level 2 has only three spots, assigned to Dr. Patel, Angela, and the Chief of Medicine — who is on a two-week vacation in Greece.
The scratch runs down the driver's side of Dr. Patel's Tesla — the side facing Angela's parking spot.

Tesla Sentry Mode recorded a blurry figure leaning against the driver's side of the car at 7:43 AM, holding a large box against the door for nearly a minute. Angela's LinkedIn post from that morning included a photo of the same box captioned 'big delivery to the office today!'

exonerations.nurse-kim: Nurse Kim's badge shows continuous presence in the ER from 5:58 AM to 2:04 PM. She didn't return to the garage during the scratch window.
exonerations.gary: Gary's booth has a camera and a log — he was in the booth taking tickets and waving cars through without a single gap longer than a bathroom break until 3 PM.
exonerations.dr-mason: Dr. Mason's surgical team confirmed he was scrubbed in from 7:55 AM to 12:20 PM, and his office keycard shows continuous occupancy until 4 PM.
exonerations.phil: Phil's visitor badge was issued at 9:03 AM and returned at 12:58 PM — well after the 7:43 AM incident.

reveal: Clue 1 narrows the people parking next to Dr. Patel to just Angela (the Chief is in Greece). Clue 2 confirms the damage came from the neighboring spot — Angela's. Clue 3 is the smoking gun: Tesla Sentry Mode caught Angela at 7:43 AM pressing a large box against Dr. Patel's car, and Angela herself posted a photo of that same box online that morning. She scraped the car while unloading and never reported it.

title: The Missing Mic
case: The open mic night wireless microphone vanished from The Blue Note bar between soundcheck at 5 PM and doors at 7 PM. It's a $400 Shure and the bar owner wants it back.

suspects.name: Charlie
suspects.alibi: I set up the mic at soundcheck, then went to grab dinner at the taco truck. I got back at 6:45 and noticed it was gone.

suspects.name: Mia
suspects.alibi: I was prepping the bar from 4 to 7. I was behind the bar the whole time — the stage is across the room from me.

suspects.name: Devon
suspects.alibi: I came in at 6 to warm up backstage. I brought my own mic because I always do — I don't trust house equipment.

suspects.name: Sam
suspects.alibi: I was doing homework in the back office from 4 to 7. I only came out to grab a soda from behind the bar at 5:30.

suspects.name: Liz
suspects.alibi: I showed up at 6:30 to sign up for a slot. I waited by the sign-up sheet near the door until it opened at 7.

starter: The bar's back door was propped open from 5:30 to 6:30 PM to air out the room. It leads to the alley behind the bar.
Devon's car was parked directly behind the back door in the alley from 6 PM onward — the only car there.

Last month, Devon specifically emailed the bar to request the house Shure mic for his set: 'that mic sounds incredible on my voice.' Tonight he told everyone he brought his own because he doesn't trust house gear.

exonerations.charlie: Charlie's taco truck receipt is timestamped 5:15 PM, and the truck is a 20-minute walk away. He couldn't have been back at the bar until close to 6:45.
exonerations.mia: Mia's bar POS system logged 43 drink orders rung up between 5:30 and 7 PM — she was behind the bar with a line of customers the entire time.
exonerations.sam: Sam's soda run at 5:30 was confirmed by Mia, who poured it. Sam went straight back to the office and the office door security cam shows no exits until 7.
exonerations.liz: Liz signed in on the sheet at 6:33 PM and chatted with the doorman for 27 minutes straight — the doorman confirms she never moved.

reveal: Clue 3 exposes Devon's lie: he claims he doesn't trust house equipment, but just last month he emailed the bar begging to use the exact mic that's now missing. He loves that mic — he didn't bring his own, he came to take this one. Clue 1 shows the back door was wide open during his arrival, and Clue 2 places his car parked right outside that door — a clean in-and-out route for walking a $400 mic to his trunk.

title: The Test Answer Leak
case: Someone photographed tomorrow's final exam and posted it to a group chat. Professor Walsh discovered the leak at 4 PM when a student reported it. The exam was only printed at 2 PM.

suspects.name: Jordan
suspects.alibi: I picked up the printed exams from the copy room at 2:15 and delivered them straight to Professor Walsh's locked office. I didn't look at them.

suspects.name: Elena
suspects.alibi: I printed the exams at 2 PM and put them in the sealed envelope Professor Walsh prepared. I handed the envelope to Jordan.

suspects.name: Marcus
suspects.alibi: I was in the library studying with my group from 1 to 5. We had a study room reserved — four people can vouch for me.

suspects.name: Prof. Lin
suspects.alibi: I was in my office next to Walsh's all afternoon. I didn't see anyone go in or out after Jordan's delivery.

suspects.name: Tanya
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk of the department office from 1 to 5. The only person who asked for Walsh's office key was Jordan at 2:20.

starter: The leaked photo's metadata shows it was taken at 2:22 PM, and it was posted to the group chat at 2:25 PM.
Jordan signed out Professor Walsh's office key at 2:20 PM and returned it at 2:35 PM — the only person to access the office all afternoon. Walsh was in a faculty meeting from 1 to 4 PM.
The sealed envelope containing the exams was found on Walsh's desk with the seal cut open. Walsh never touched it — she was in her meeting when Jordan delivered it.

exonerations.elena: Elena's copy room badge log shows she didn't leave the copy room between 1 and 3 PM, and she handed the sealed envelope off without opening it (Jordan's signed receipt confirms this).
exonerations.marcus-t: Marcus's library study room booking and keycard access log confirm continuous presence from 12:55 PM to 5:10 PM, with four witnesses.
exonerations.professor-lin: Prof. Lin's office is keycard-locked and logged — she never left her office from 12:45 PM to 4:15 PM, and her door faces Walsh's (she'd have been seen going in).
exonerations.tanya: Tanya's front-desk log tracks every key sign-out. She never left her post, and the only key handed out for Walsh's office was Jordan's at 2:20.

reveal: Clue 2 places Jordan inside Walsh's locked office from 2:20 to 2:35 PM — the only person who had access all afternoon. Clue 1 pinpoints the photo's capture at 2:22 PM, right inside that 15-minute window. Clue 3 shows the sealed envelope was cut open, but Walsh never touched it — she was still in her meeting. Jordan had the only opportunity, opened the envelope, photographed the exam, and posted it three minutes later.

title: The Gym Bag Switcheroo
case: Someone swapped Pete's gym bag (containing his wallet, watch, and AirPods) with an identical empty bag in the locker room between 6 and 7 AM during his workout.

suspects.name: Coach Ray
suspects.alibi: I was on the gym floor training a client from 5:45 to 7. I never went into the men's locker room during that time.

suspects.name: Dennis
suspects.alibi: I was doing my usual cardio routine on the treadmill from 6 to 6:45. I showered and left by 7:10.

suspects.name: Lisa
suspects.alibi: I was at the front desk the whole morning. I don't go into the locker rooms — that's not my job.

suspects.name: Wayne
suspects.alibi: I did weights from 6:15 to 7. I used the locker next to Pete's. I didn't see anything suspicious.

suspects.name: Hank
suspects.alibi: I was cleaning the pool area from 6 to 7:30. The pool is on the other side of the building from the locker rooms.

starter: The gym's check-in system shows Wayne arrived at 5:50 AM — 25 minutes before the 6:15 time he claimed he started lifting.
The pro shop sold one identical bag to Pete's at 5:55 AM this morning, paid in cash. The transaction was processed by Lisa. The pro shop is a short walk from the men's locker room.
Wayne's assigned locker — used daily — is directly next to Pete's.

exonerations.coach-ray: Coach Ray's client confirmed a full uninterrupted session from 5:45 to 7 AM on the gym floor, visible on the gym's main-floor camera the whole time.
exonerations.dennis: Dennis's treadmill data log shows continuous activity from 6:02 to 6:44 AM with no pauses longer than 30 seconds — not enough time to enter the locker room.
exonerations.lisa: Lisa processed 47 check-ins and a pro shop sale from the front desk between 5:30 and 7:30 AM — she never left her post.
exonerations.hank: Hank's keycard log shows him in the pool area from 5:58 to 7:32 AM, and the pool deck cam has him in frame continuously.

reveal: Clue 1 catches Wayne in a 25-minute lie about when he arrived. Clue 2 reveals why: at 5:55 AM — five minutes after Wayne showed up — an identical gym bag was bought from the pro shop in cash. Clue 3 places Wayne in the locker right next to Pete's, giving him a perfect setup. Wayne bought the decoy, waited for Pete to head to the gym floor, and swapped the bags. His '6:15 start time' was a cover for the 25 minutes he actually needed.

title: The Potluck Saboteur
case: Someone dumped an entire shaker of salt into Rita's award-winning chili at the neighborhood potluck. The chili was set out at 5 PM and declared inedible by 5:30 PM.

suspects.name: Barb
suspects.alibi: I was at the dessert table the whole time arranging my seven-layer bars. I was at the opposite end of the food line from the chili.

suspects.name: Greg
suspects.alibi: I was running around setting up tables and chairs. I didn't go near the food once — I hadn't even grabbed a plate yet.

suspects.name: Yolanda
suspects.alibi: I was helping Rita carry dishes from her car. I set down the cornbread right next to the chili and then went to park.

suspects.name: Neil
suspects.alibi: I was watching the kids on the bounce house from 4:45 to 5:30. My wife waved at me the whole time.

suspects.name: Dot
suspects.alibi: I was the first person to taste the chili at 5:15. It was already salty by then! I immediately told Rita something was wrong.

starter: Three neighbors served themselves chili between 5:00 and 5:10 PM and all said it tasted perfect. The first complaint was Dot's at 5:15.
The salt shaker next to the chili was nearly empty; the one at the other end of the food table was still full. The sabotage happened at the chili end of the table.
A neighbor's photo at 5:12 PM captured Dot standing alone at the chili pot — the only person at the food table at that moment — holding the salt shaker.

exonerations.rita-neighbor: Barb's seven-layer bars were being photographed by two neighbors at the dessert table from 5:05 to 5:20 — she was in every photo, posing with her tray.
exonerations.greg: Greg was captured on a neighbor's doorbell camera carrying folding chairs at 5:12 PM at the far end of the yard, 40 feet from the chili.
exonerations.yolanda: Yolanda's car was parked three blocks away. Neighbors confirm she didn't return from parking until 5:20 PM — after the chili was already salted.
exonerations.neil: Neil was at the bounce house from 4:45 to 5:30 continuously; his wife and four other parents confirm they saw him there without interruption.

reveal: Clue 1 narrows the sabotage window to 5:10–5:15 (the chili was fine through 5:10; Dot 'discovered' it salty at 5:15). Clue 2 locates the tampering at the chili end of the table. Clue 3 is the smoking gun — a photo at 5:12 PM shows Dot standing alone at the chili pot holding the salt shaker. She salted the pot and then immediately 'found' the problem to cast herself as the concerned taster instead of the saboteur.

title: The Dog Park Dognapping
case: Someone walked off with Olivia's French Bulldog, Biscuit, from the Riverside Dog Park at approximately 3:30 PM while Olivia was refilling water bowls at the far end of the park.

suspects.name: Paul
suspects.alibi: I had three dogs with me from 3 to 4 PM. I was in the large dog section the whole time. Biscuit is a small dog — she'd be in the other section.

suspects.name: Trish
suspects.alibi: I was chatting with Maria by the benches near the entrance from 3:15 to 3:45. My poodle Coco was playing off-leash.

suspects.name: Evan
suspects.alibi: I was jogging the loop trail around the park. I passed through but I don't stop — I was doing intervals and had my headphones in.

suspects.name: Maria
suspects.alibi: I was talking to Trish on the bench by the entrance from 3:15 to 3:45. My Lab, Bruno, was right at my feet.

suspects.name: Noah
suspects.alibi: I just moved to the neighborhood. I was at the park from 3 to 4 scoping it out for my puppy. I don't even have a leash with me.

starter: A park bench camera captured someone walking out the side gate at 3:32 PM carrying a small French Bulldog in a blue puffer jacket — Biscuit's signature outfit.
The side gate is locked from the large dog section and only accessible from inside the small dog section. A small-dog collar leash was found dropped at the gate.
Noah was the only adult in the small dog section without a dog of his own. He told Olivia at 3:10 PM that he 'really loves French Bulldogs' and asked if Biscuit was friendly.

exonerations.paul: Paul's three client dogs are all over 50 pounds and legally separated into the large dog section by the double-gate rule. He never crossed into the small dog section.
exonerations.trish: Trish was visible to the park bench camera on the entrance benches from 3:15 to 3:45, with her poodle Coco in frame the entire time.
exonerations.evan: Evan's fitness tracker GPS shows he was on the outer loop trail at 3:30 PM, outside the fenced dog park. He never entered.
exonerations.maria: Maria was sitting next to Trish on the entrance bench from 3:15 to 3:45, confirmed on the bench cam, with her Lab Bruno lying at her feet the entire time.

reveal: Clue 1 shows someone leaving with Biscuit in her distinctive jacket at 3:32. Clue 2 proves the culprit had to be inside the small dog section and dropped a leash at the exit gate — despite Noah claiming he had no leash. Clue 3 is the smoking gun: Noah was the only adult in the small dog section without a dog of his own, and he'd approached Olivia minutes earlier asking about Biscuit specifically. He came prepared with a leash, scouted his target, and slipped out the side gate.

title: The Dorm Room DJ
case: Someone blasted music from Room 412 at 3 AM, waking up the entire fourth floor. The room belongs to Casey, who insists they were asleep and their speaker was hijacked via Bluetooth.

suspects.name: Casey
suspects.alibi: I was dead asleep. My speaker was still on from studying earlier. Someone must have connected to it — it doesn't have a password.

suspects.name: Riley
suspects.alibi: I was up late working on a paper. I heard the music start and immediately went to Casey's door to complain, but nobody answered.

suspects.name: Alex
suspects.alibi: I was FaceTiming my partner until 2:30 AM, then went to sleep. The music woke me up at 3.

suspects.name: Jamie
suspects.alibi: I got back from a late study session at the library around 2:45. I was brushing my teeth when the music started.

suspects.name: Morgan
suspects.alibi: I was asleep in my room. I'm a heavy sleeper — I didn't hear anything until Riley came banging on my door at 3:10.

starter: Casey's Bluetooth speaker has a range of about 30 feet. Only rooms 410, 411, 413, and 414 are within range of Room 412. Any other room is too far to connect.

The music played was from a Spotify playlist called 'Revenge Mix.' The playlist was created two days ago by an account named 'R1leyPlays' — Riley's gaming username, which Riley has used publicly on their social media for years.
Riley has filed three formal noise complaints against Casey in the past month.

exonerations.casey: Casey's phone sleep tracker recorded continuous deep sleep from 1:15 to 3:02 AM, and their door camera shows them in bed asleep when the music started — they physically couldn't have triggered it.
exonerations.alex-k: Alex's room (415) is out of Bluetooth range from Room 412 — too far down the hall to connect to Casey's speaker.
exonerations.jamie: Jamie's room (414) is in range, but their library keycard shows they returned at 2:47 AM and their door cam shows they didn't leave their room after. Their Spotify account was also inactive that night.
exonerations.morgan: Morgan's room (401) is on the opposite end of the floor, out of Bluetooth range. Their RA duty log also shows no activity between 11 PM and 3:10 AM.

reveal: Clue 1 limits the culprit to someone in Room 410, 411, 413, or 414. Clue 3 establishes motive — Riley has been feuding with Casey over noise for a month. Clue 2 is the smoking gun: the 'Revenge Mix' playlist was created under Riley's public gaming username. Riley connected to Casey's unsecured speaker from Room 410 next door, blasted the playlist, then played the outraged neighbor to frame Casey.

title: The Wedding Gift Grab
case: An envelope containing $500 cash disappeared from the wedding gift table at the Riverside Inn reception. The bride noticed it missing during the card opening at 10 PM. It was placed on the table at 6 PM.

suspects.name: Uncle Rob
suspects.alibi: I was at the bar most of the night. I put my card on the table at 6 when I arrived and then hit the dance floor around 8.

suspects.name: Claire
suspects.alibi: I was coordinating everything all night. I checked the gift table at 7 PM to make sure it looked nice, but I didn't touch any envelopes.

suspects.name: Dante
suspects.alibi: I was busing tables and restocking the bar. I walked past the gift table a few times but I had my hands full of dishes.

suspects.name: Megan
suspects.alibi: I was with the bridal party doing photos from 5 to 7, then at the head table for dinner, then dancing. I barely left the main room.

suspects.name: Tyler
suspects.alibi: I arrived late around 8:30 because my flight was delayed. I dropped my card on the table and went to find the groom.

starter: The venue's entrance log (every guest scanned a QR invite on arrival) shows Tyler checked in at 7:15 PM — over an hour before he claims he arrived.
Tyler's flight landed at 2 PM local time, four hours before the ceremony. There was no delay — the groom confirmed Tyler texted from baggage claim at 2:30 PM saying he was heading to his hotel.

The $500 envelope from the groom's boss was distinctive: thick, with a gold foil company logo. A hallway camera caught Tyler pocketing a matching gold-foil envelope from the gift table at 7:24 PM, then walking toward the restrooms.

exonerations.uncle-rob: Uncle Rob's bar tab shows continuous drink orders from 6:15 PM to 9:45 PM. The bartender remembers him fondly — he didn't leave the bar area until dancing.
exonerations.claire: Claire's coordinator assistant was with her during the 7 PM gift table check, and the gift table is on the hallway camera — she didn't touch any envelope.
exonerations.dante: Dante's manager confirmed he was on dish duty in the kitchen from 7:00 to 8:45 PM. The kitchen camera shows continuous presence — he never stepped into the hallway.
exonerations.megan: Megan is in every bridal party photo from 5 to 7, then seated at the head table — which faces the gift table — from 7 to 9:30 PM, visible to 120 guests.

reveal: Clue 1 shows Tyler actually arrived at 7:15 PM — not 8:30. Clue 2 proves the 'delayed flight' was a lie: he landed at 2 and texted from baggage claim. Clue 3 is the smoking gun: the hallway camera caught him pocketing the gold-foil envelope from the gift table at 7:24 PM. He arrived early, waited for a quiet moment, grabbed the biggest-looking envelope, and later acted like he'd just walked in the door.
